uses: Triolena obliqua has been traditionally used in folk medicine in South America, particularly in the Andean region. It is reported to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is sometimes used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. In some regions, the leaves of Triolena obliqua are used to make a medicinal tea, which is said to have antipyretic and expectorant properties. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Triolena obliqua for medicinal purposes.
